title: aquilaria ophispermum
intro: Aquilaria ophispermum is a species of agarwood tree in the family Thymelaeaceae, native to Southeast Asia, known for its unique and highly valued resinous wood, often referred to as "gagal" or "aguilar". The medicinal properties of Aquilaria ophispermum are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. Aquilaria ophispermum is often used in herbal preparations, including essential oils, infusions, and traditional remedies, which are typically administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
